# ZQUAS

> Sovereign compliance infrastructure for regulated finance. GPU-native compliance engine built for cross-institutional financial crime detection without central data pooling.

ZQUAS is a GPU-native compliance engine built for financial institutions and regulators. The F1 engine evaluates 500,000 entities against 100 AML policies in under 2 seconds, sustaining 150 million+ compliance policy evaluations per second on NVIDIA RTX 5090. Alert lifecycle under 10ms. Cross-institutional detection uses privacy-preserving multi-party computation: ECDH-PSI (X25519) for entity matching, Yao's Garbled Circuits (Free-XOR) for risk comparison, IKNP OT Extension for oblivious transfer. Every compliance decision produces a cryptographic proof bundle (912-byte ZAPB, Ed25519 signed, Merkle-included) that regulators verify independently with a public key alone. 5,000+ automated tests across 10 audited subsystems.

Founded by Danny de Gier, who combines 18+ years of financial crime compliance experience at Tier-1 banks (RBS, Deutsche Bank, HSBC, Commerzbank) with GPU systems programming (C++23, CUDA, Vulkan). Currently engaged with DNB InnovationHub and FCA regulatory sandbox programmes.

- [Articles](https://zquas.ai/articles.html)

## Core Capabilities

- GPU-native compliance engine (not GPU-accelerated, GPU-native)
- 150M+ CEPS (compliance policy evaluations per second) on NVIDIA RTX 5090
- 500K entities × 100 AML policies in under 2 seconds; alert lifecycle under 10ms
- 29 AML policies (100 rules, 6 domains) + 15 access control policies
- Deterministic evaluation: same input + same policy = same verdict, byte-identical replay
- Privacy-preserving federation: ECDH-PSI (X25519) + Yao's GC (Free-XOR) + IKNP OT; semi-honest security model
- ~15 seconds per bilateral round at 100K entities (TCP localhost); dual Ed25519 attestation per round
- Cryptographic proof bundles for every compliance decision (Ed25519, Merkle roots, BLAKE3)
- Independent regulator verification via standalone CLI (no vendor software needed)
- Zero-knowledge governance proofs (GPU-accelerated PLONK)
- GPU-resident identity resolution graph with GNN risk propagation
- Adversarial self-testing framework (13+ subsystems, GPU-accelerated fuzzing)
- Predictive governance via Janus projection layer (forward simulation of compliance state)
- Constitutional governance model (ExecutionGate, WarrantAuthority, GasMeter, PolicyRoot)
- Build attestation with embedded BLAKE3 + SHA-256 + Ed25519 binary integrity verification

## Regulatory Framework Coverage

- EU AI Act (Articles 9, 11, 12, 14)
- AMLR / 6AMLD (effective mid-2027)
- FATF Recommendation 15
- NIST AI RMF
- ISO 42001
- MAS TRM
- GDPR (privacy-preserving by architecture)
- DORA (digital operational resilience)

## Competitive Positioning

- Legacy incumbents (NICE Actimize, Oracle, SAS): CPU batch processing, no MPC, no cryptographic proofs
- AI-native challengers (Sardine, Hawk AI, Featurespace, ComplyAdvantage): CPU cloud, ML-based, no cross-institutional detection
- ZQUAS: GPU-native, MPC cross-institutional, cryptographic attestation, deterministic, on-premise sovereign
- Estimated replication timeline for competitors: 3-5 years minimum
